description: Native app surfaces phase A6 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the mobile interaction vocabulary on Android. swipe (directional-object and point-to-point forms) drives mobile: swipeGesture / W3C touch actions; find auto-scrolls (mobile: scrollGesture) to locate an entry deep in the Settings list; click.duration long-presses (mobile: longClickGesture); and a criteria-less device-key press ($BACK$) goes through mobile: pressKey. Settings is the target app because it is preinstalled on every Android system image, and the steps stay robust across API levels (find-by-list-entry, not by a specific input widget). Criteria-less focused text typing (mobile: type) can't reach a guaranteed-focusable field in stock Settings across images, so it is covered by unit tests (test/type-mobile-keys.test.js) rather than here. SKIPPED on hosts without emulator acceleration — the real PASS coverage runs on the KVM legs.

tests.description: The directional and point-to-point swipe forms on the Settings list (the bare string form is exercised on browser surfaces, where a surface-less step has an active tab to act on), then find an entry near the bottom ('System') that requires auto-scroll, long-press it, and press the device Back key with no element criteria. The long-press gesture itself is the assertion — Settings ignores long-clicks, so the UI stays wherever the auto-scroll left it.
